problem.title_line1: Your brain struggles to write and
problem.subtitle: Lectures move fast. Notes fall behind. You end up choosing between understanding and capturing — and either way, something gets lost.

items.title: Hand cramps, missed words
items.desc: Writing fast enough to keep up means your notes are incomplete — or illegible.

items.title: ADHD makes it harder
items.desc: Splitting focus between listening and writing is exhausting. Something always slips.

items.title: Non-native language
items.desc: Following a fast lecture in your second language is double the cognitive load.

items.title: No notes = no studying
items.desc: Without written notes, reviewing means watching the whole lecture again.

origin.lede: We're Victoria and Pierre — a couple from the south of France. Victoria is studying anthropology online — every lecture, every seminar, every workshop streamed from home. Pierre loves building things with code. This started as a side project to see if a little Chrome extension could help — and turned into something we think everyone deserves.
origin.p1: Victoria's days are hours of video lectures back to back. Taking notes while watching meant losing the thread. Rewinding, rewriting, rewatching. Notes too sparse to study from, or too rushed to read. On top of that, she has ADHD — which makes splitting focus between listening and writing not just hard, but exhausting.
origin.p2: So Pierre started building, and Victoria tested every version — broke things, gave feedback, explained what was actually missing. Evening after evening, laptop to laptop.
origin.close: This is the result. We're making it available to everyone because no student should have to choose between listening and writing. If it helps you even once, it was worth it.

who.title: Built for people who learn by reading along

items.title: Students
items.desc: University lectures, online courses, MOOCs — capture everything without losing the thread of what's being said.

items.title: ADHD Learners
items.desc: Reading along while listening dramatically reduces cognitive load. Stop losing focus mid-lecture.

items.title: Non-native speakers
items.desc: Watching lectures in a second language? Having the transcript appear in real-time lets you follow along without missing a word.

items.title: Online Course Takers
items.desc: Udemy, Coursera, YouTube tutorials — turn any video into searchable, editable notes automatically.

items.title: Accuracy you can trust
items.desc: Audio is sent in chunks — 10 seconds in Normal mode, 30 seconds in Optimal for even better accuracy. A short delay, but far more accurate text than any real-time alternative.

items.title: A real note editor
items.desc: Don't just read — work directly in the transcript. Highlight key phrases, bold terms, add headers and bullets, remove the parts you don't need. Most students finish their notes before the lecture ends.

items.title: Export anywhere
items.desc: Download as .txt or .md, or copy to clipboard. Your notes, your format.

items.title: 14 languages
items.desc: English, French, Spanish, Arabic, Hindi and more. Transcribe in the language of your lecture — great for non-native speakers following along.

items.title: Privacy first
items.desc: Everything stays local by default — no account, no cloud processing in free mode. Your audio, your data.

items.title: Session history
items.desc: All your past transcriptions are saved automatically. Pick up where you left off — nothing is ever lost.

worksEverywhere.subtitle: University internal platforms, protected videos, public sites — if audio comes out of your computer, we can transcribe it.

platforms.title: University platforms
platforms.desc: Moodle, Canvas, Blackboard, Panopto — the ones with no download, no subtitles, and no help. If the video plays in your browser, we transcribe it.

platforms.title: Public video sites
platforms.desc: YouTube, Coursera, Udemy — all the classics. But also any embedded player on any site, no matter how obscure or non-standard.

platforms.title: Works with headphones
platforms.desc: AI mode captures tab audio directly from the browser — no microphone, no speakers needed. Study at 2am without disturbing anyone.

worksEverywhere.explainerEyebrow: How the two modes capture audio
worksEverywhere.explainerFree: Free mode uses your microphone to pick up audio through your speakers. Works on every platform — but volume matters. Turn system volume up for best accuracy.
worksEverywhere.explainerAI: AI mode captures the browser tab audio directly, bypassing your mic entirely. Clean capture with headphones on, at any volume, on any protected platform.

pricing.subtitle: Start for free with your microphone. Top up when you need AI-powered transcription — no subscription, no expiry.
pricing.whyPay: Why does AI mode cost money?
pricing.whyPayDesc: Free mode uses your browser's built-in speech recognition — local, instant, free. AI mode sends your audio to a state-of-the-art cloud AI model. That processing has a real cost. We pass it on transparently: credits are consumed second-by-second from your pack, they never expire, and you choose how fast you burn them based on the transcription mode you use.
pricing.freeDesc: Uses your browser's built-in speech engine and your microphone. Fully local — no audio sent anywhere.
freeFeatures: Full editing — highlights, bold, headers, bullets
pricing.freeCaveat: Audio must play through your speakers — the mic needs to hear it.
pricing.premiumLabel: AI Mode — Pay as you go
pricing.noSubCallout: No subscription. No "I forgot to cancel" tax. Credits never expire.
pricing.costCallout: Buy a pack once. Your hours are consumed second-by-second as you transcribe, so nothing is wasted when you stop. The bigger the pack, the lower the price per hour.
pricing.headphonesCallout: Works with headphones on. Tab audio is captured directly — no microphone, no speakers needed.
premiumFeatures: Everything in Free, including full editing
Cloud AI engine — far better accuracy than browser speech
Direct tab audio — works with any headphones
Higher accuracy across accents & background noise
Hours consumed second-by-second — nothing wasted when you stop
Credits never expire — top up whenever you want
10-min grace period if credits run out mid-lecture
pricing.currencyDisclaimer: Prices shown are estimates. Final amount is converted at the live exchange rate by Lemon Squeezy at checkout.
pricing.paymentProcessor: Payments processed securely by Lemon Squeezy.
pricing.footnote: Buy once, use whenever. A full semester at 3h/week costs around $21 — less than two textbook chapters.

items.q: How do credits work?
items.a: Buy a credit pack and you'll receive a license key by email. Open the extension settings, enter the key, and your credits activate immediately. They're consumed second-by-second as you transcribe — nothing is wasted when you stop.

items.q: I lost my activation code. What do I do?
items.a: Check your inbox — the key was sent when you purchased. Your key is portable: it works on any computer, survives reinstalls, and your remaining credits are always preserved. Think of it as a prepaid balance, not a device licence.

items.q: My transcription is in the wrong language.
items.a: Make sure you've selected the correct language in the extension settings before starting. If you changed language mid-session, stop the transcription, select the right language, and start again. The speech engine needs a clean restart to switch languages.

items.q: The transcription isn't working at all.
items.a: Try these steps in order: (1) Make sure the extension is up to date — check for updates in chrome://extensions. (2) Reload the extension by toggling it off and on. (3) Reload the page with the video. (4) Check your internet connection — AI mode requires it. (5) If you're using mic mode, make sure your system volume is high enough (80%+) and that you've granted the extension permission to access your microphone.

items.q: Microphone mode isn't picking up the audio.
items.a: This is almost always a volume issue. Free mode relies on your mic hearing your speakers — the louder your system volume, the better the pickup. MacBook Air users typically need a higher volume than MacBook Pro users. Also make sure you've allowed microphone access for the extension.

items.q: Can I transcribe multiple videos at the same time?
items.a: Yes — but only with AI mode. AI mode captures tab audio per tab, so each video runs independently. Free (mic) mode captures whatever your speakers play, so mixing audio sources will confuse the transcription.

youtube.h1: Transcribe Any YouTube Video in Real-Time
youtube.subtitle: Get a live, editable transcript of any YouTube video directly in Chrome — without copying, pasting, or switching tabs. Free mode included.
youtube.answer: To transcribe a YouTube video in Chrome: install the LessonScriptor extension, open your YouTube video, click the side panel icon, and hit play. Transcription starts automatically and appears in real-time alongside the video.

methods.title: How to get a YouTube video transcript — all methods compared

items.cons: Read-only, no editing, not available on all videos, inaccurate on auto-generated captions

items.pros: Live editing, highlight, export, works on any video not just YouTube, 14+ languages, free mode

items.cons: No live transcription, not real-time, no editing, YouTube-only

steps.title: Step-by-step: transcribe a YouTube video with LessonScriptor
items: Install LessonScriptor from the Chrome Web Store (free, one click, no sign-up).
Open any YouTube video in Chrome.
Click the LessonScriptor icon in your Chrome toolbar to open the side panel.
Hit play on the video — transcription starts automatically.
Edit as you watch: click to add notes, highlight important passages, delete tangents.
Export to Markdown when done — your formatted notes are ready.

items.q: Can I download a transcript from a YouTube video?
items.a: Yes. With LessonScriptor, you can export the transcript as a Markdown file (.md) or copy it to clipboard to paste into Notion, Google Docs, or Obsidian. The export preserves all your formatting — bold, highlights, headers.

items.q: Does it work on YouTube videos without captions?
items.a: Yes. LessonScriptor doesn't rely on YouTube's captions — it transcribes the actual audio in real-time. So it works whether or not YouTube has auto-generated captions for the video.

items.q: Can I transcribe a YouTube video in French or Spanish?
items.a: Yes. LessonScriptor supports 14+ languages. In the extension settings, select your target language and it will transcribe accordingly — useful for language learning or international lectures.

items.q: Is there a limit to how long a YouTube video can be?
items.a: No hard limit. LessonScriptor transcribes in real-time as the video plays, so it works for any length — from a 5-minute tutorial to a 3-hour lecture.

liveCaptions.h1: Live Captions for Chrome — Editable, Exportable, Multilingual
liveCaptions.subtitle: Add real-time, editable captions to any video you watch in Chrome. Free, private, and works on every platform — not just YouTube.
liveCaptions.answer: LessonScriptor adds live AI captions to any video in Chrome via a side panel. Unlike Chrome's built-in live captions, these are fully editable, exportable, and work with 14+ languages — including academic vocabulary your browser's captions get wrong.

vsChrome.title: LessonScriptor vs Chrome's built-in Live Captions

steps.title: How to add live captions in Chrome — step by step
items: Install LessonScriptor from the Chrome Web Store. It's free, takes 10 seconds, no sign-up required.
Open any video in Chrome — YouTube, Coursera, a Zoom recording, Google Meet, any platform.
Click the LessonScriptor icon in your Chrome toolbar to open the side panel next to the video.
Hit play on the video. Live captions appear in real-time inside the side panel automatically.
Click anywhere in the transcript to edit, correct a word, or add a note as you watch.
Use highlights to mark key moments — 5 colours, bold, headers to structure your notes.
Export when done — copy to clipboard or download as Markdown, ready for Notion, Obsidian, or Google Docs.

useCases.title: What you can use Chrome live captions for

items.title: University lectures
items.description: Follow along with recorded or live lectures without missing words. Edit the transcript in real-time, highlight key concepts, and export structured notes when the lecture ends.

items.title: YouTube educational videos
items.description: Transcribe any YouTube tutorial, course, or documentary as it plays. No need to pause and retype — just highlight what matters and skip the rest.

items.title: Zoom and Teams recordings
items.description: LessonScriptor works on any video that plays in a Chrome tab — including Zoom cloud recordings and Teams videos. Get captions even when no transcript was generated.

items.title: Language learning
items.description: Watch videos in a foreign language and get live captions in that language. Supports French, Spanish, German, Portuguese, Mandarin, and 9 more languages — useful for accent comprehension and vocabulary.

items.title: ADHD and accessibility
items.description: Reading captions while listening significantly improves comprehension and retention for students with ADHD, auditory processing difficulties, or those in noisy environments.

items.title: Online courses and webinars
items.description: Transcribe Coursera, edX, Udemy, or any live webinar in Chrome. Export your notes after the session with all your edits and highlights preserved.

items.q: How do I add live captions to Chrome?
items.a: Install LessonScriptor from the Chrome Web Store. Open any video, click the extension icon to open the side panel, and hit play. Live captions appear automatically in real-time.

items.q: Does Chrome have live captions built in?
items.a: Yes, Chrome has a basic live captions feature under Settings → Accessibility → Live Caption. However, it only supports English, produces a read-only feed you can't edit or export, and doesn't handle technical vocabulary well. LessonScriptor supports 14+ languages and gives you a fully editable, exportable transcript.

items.q: How do I turn on live captions in Chrome for free?
items.a: Two options: Chrome's built-in live captions are free but English-only and not editable (Settings → Accessibility → Live Caption). LessonScriptor also has a free mode — install from the Chrome Web Store and it works immediately with no account required. The free mode uses your device's microphone for transcription.

items.q: Do Chrome live captions work with headphones?
items.a: Chrome's built-in live captions do not work with headphones because they rely on the microphone. LessonScriptor's Premium mode uses tab audio capture — it grabs audio directly from the browser tab rather than through a microphone, so headphones work perfectly.

items.q: Can I get live captions in languages other than English in Chrome?
items.a: Chrome's built-in captions are English-only. LessonScriptor supports 14+ languages including French, Spanish, German, Portuguese, Mandarin, Italian, Dutch, Polish, and more. Select your language in the extension settings before starting transcription.

items.q: Why are Chrome's built-in live captions inaccurate?
items.a: Chrome's live captions use an older on-device speech recognition model optimised for general speech, not academic or technical vocabulary. LessonScriptor's Premium mode uses a more accurate AI model and supports a personal vocabulary dictionary — you can add names, terms, and abbreviations it should always get right.

items.q: Can I export Chrome live captions?
items.a: Chrome's built-in live captions cannot be exported or saved — the text disappears when the video stops. LessonScriptor saves your full transcript as you go and lets you export to Markdown, copy to clipboard, or keep it in the extension for later.

items.q: Do Chrome live captions work on Zoom recordings?
items.a: Chrome's built-in live captions work on any audio playing in the browser, including Zoom recordings. LessonScriptor also works on any video playing in a Chrome tab, with the added benefit of an editable, exportable transcript.

items.q: Are Chrome live captions private?
items.a: Chrome's built-in live captions process audio on-device — nothing leaves your computer. LessonScriptor's free mode also uses on-device speech recognition. The Premium mode sends audio to a cloud API for better accuracy, but no data is stored after transcription.

transcribeVideo.h1: Transcribe Video to Text — Free, in Your Browser
transcribeVideo.subtitle: Convert any video to text in real-time with AI. No upload, no waiting, no subscription. Works directly in Chrome on any video platform.
transcribeVideo.answer: To transcribe video to text for free in Chrome: install LessonScriptor, open any video, and hit play. AI transcribes the audio in real-time and displays it as editable text in a side panel. Free mode is free forever.

items.q: How do I transcribe a video to text for free?
items.a: Install LessonScriptor (free Chrome extension). Open any video in Chrome. Click the extension icon to open the side panel. Press play — transcription starts automatically. Export the text when done. Free mode is free forever with no sign-up.

items.q: Can I convert an MP4 to text?
items.a: Yes. Open the MP4 file in Chrome (drag and drop into a Chrome tab, or open via File → Open), then use LessonScriptor to transcribe it in real-time as it plays.

items.q: What is the most accurate free video transcription tool?
items.a: For live, in-browser transcription, LessonScriptor's Premium mode offers the highest accuracy. The Free mode uses the Web Speech API, which is good for general content. Both are available without a subscription.

items.q: How long does it take to transcribe a video?
items.a: LessonScriptor transcribes in real-time — the text appears as the video plays, so there's no waiting. A 1-hour video takes 1 hour to transcribe.

otterAlt.h1: The Best Otter.ai Alternative for Students
otterAlt.subtitle: Looking for an Otter.ai alternative that works in your browser, doesn't require a subscription, and is free for everyday use? That's LessonScriptor.
otterAlt.answer: LessonScriptor is the best Otter.ai alternative for students because it works in Chrome on any video platform (not just meetings), has a free mode with no limits, and charges only ~€1/hour for premium — no monthly subscription.

items.q: What is the best free alternative to Otter.ai?
items.a: LessonScriptor is the best free Otter.ai alternative for students. Its free mode is free forever with no monthly limit — it uses your microphone to capture audio from any video in Chrome. No subscription, no sign-up required.

items.q: Is there an Otter.ai alternative that works on YouTube?
items.a: Yes — LessonScriptor. Unlike Otter.ai, which is designed for meetings and calls, LessonScriptor works on any video in Chrome, including YouTube, Coursera, Udemy, Zoom recordings, and university portals.

items.q: Why is Otter.ai expensive for students?
items.a: Otter.ai's Pro plan starts at $17/month. For a student who only needs to transcribe lectures during the semester, that adds up to $100+ per year. LessonScriptor's premium mode costs about €1/hour with no subscription — a full semester of 12 hours costs around €12.
